headlines.title: IEA Sees Massive Oil Surplus In 2027 As Middle East Supply Returns
headlines.summary: The global oil market could swing from one of the largest supply disruptions in history to a surplus of more than 5 million barrels per day next year if Middle East production and exports recover following the U.S.-Iran peace agreement, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said

headlines.title: Artificial Intelligence Is Driving a Geothermal Energy Boom
headlines.summary: The artificial intelligence boom is fueling an energy revolution. The extreme electricity demands of the fast-growing sector are kicking the research and development of a huge number of next-gen energy alternatives into overdrive, with particular attention to clean energy sources

headlines.title: Exxon Set to Supply LNG to South Africa’s First Import Terminal
headlines.summary: ExxonMobil and the company developing South Africa’s first LNG import terminal have signed a preliminary agreement for the U.S. supermajor to supply LNG to the proposed import terminal at the Port of Richards Bay, Zululand Energy Terminal (ZET) said on Wednesday. headlines.title: TotalEnergies: Saudi Refinery Won’t Fully Recover Until 2027
headlines.summary: A major Saudi Arabian refinery damaged during the Iran conflict will not return to full operations until early 2027, according to TotalEnergies CEO Patrick Pouyanné, raising fresh concerns about the pace of recovery in global fuel markets even as a U.S.-Iran peace agreement moves

headlines.title: US Crude Oil, Gasoline Inventories Still Falling: EIA
headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 8.3 million barrels during the week ending June 12, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The decrease brings commercial stockpiles to 418.2 million barrels, accor

headlines.title: Poland Moves To Tax Fuel Windfalls Earned During Iran War
headlines.summary: Poland’s government has approved a one-off windfall tax on fuel companies that benefited from soaring energy prices during the U.S.-Iran-Israel war, seeking to recover part of the billions spent protecting consumers from higher fuel costs. The proposed levy would impose a 60% tax

headlines.title: ECB: Iran Peace Deal Won't Erase Europe's Energy Price Shock
headlines.summary: Europe will have to contend with the energy price shock for months despite the tentative U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, European Central Bank (ECB) officials said this week. headlines.title: First Iranian Oil Moves Past U.S. Blockade Ahead of Deal Signing
headlines.summary: Iran’s first observed crude oil exports in two months have moved past the U.S. blockade outside the Strait of Hormuz in a sign that Iran is wasting no time to take advantage of the tentative deal with the United States. headlines.title: Global Gas Markets Get Another Boost as Australian LNG Strike Ends
headlines.summary: A weeks-long strike action at the Ichthys LNG facilities in Australia is coming to an end after trade unions and Japanese energy company Inpex reached an agreement on pay and benefits. headlines.title: Qatar Prepares LNG Comeback Ahead of Hormuz Reopening
headlines.summary: Qatar has started to return LNG tankers back to the Middle East in anticipation of an imminent reopening of the Strait of Hormuz that would allow the world’s second-biggest LNG exporter to start producing and moving LNG supply again. At least four Qatar-owned tankers have headed
